claimText: The threshold for availing safe harbour rules under the Income-Tax Rules has been increased from Rs. 200 Crore to Rs. 300 Crore.
evidenceExcerpt: The threshold for availing safe harbour was increased from Rs. 200 Crore to Rs. 300 Crore.
examUse: Statement-based question on recent amendments to safe harbour rules by the CBDT.
distractorHooks: Threshold remains unchanged at Rs. 200 Crore

claimText: Lithium-Ion Batteries for electric or hybrid vehicles are included in the definition of core auto components for safe harbour rules.
evidenceExcerpt: Lithium-Ion Batteries for electric/hybrid vehicles are now included in the definition of core auto components for safe harbour.
examUse: Statement-based question on items covered under core auto components for taxation purposes.
distractorHooks: Only applicable to internal combustion engine components

claimText: Safe harbour rules imply that the tax authority accepts the transfer price declared by the taxpayer as being at arm's length.
evidenceExcerpt: Safe harbour means the tax authority accepts the transfer price declared by the taxpayer to be at arm's length.
distractorHooks: Tax authority mandates a third-party audit for the declared price
Tax authority applies a fixed penalty on the declared price
Taxpayer is exempt from paying any corporate tax

claimText: An Advance Pricing Agreement (APA) determines the arm's length price of international transactions for a maximum term of 5 years with no minimum period.
evidenceExcerpt: An Advance Pricing Agreement (APA) determines the arm's length price of international transactions for a maximum term of 5 years with no minimum period.
examUse: Statement-based question on the features and tenure of Advance Pricing Agreements.
distractorHooks: Minimum term of 3 years is mandatory

claimText: Transfer Pricing refers to the pricing of goods and services exchanged between companies under common ownership or control.
evidenceExcerpt: Transfer Pricing refers to the price of goods and services exchanged between companies under common ownership or control.
examUse: Concept-based question on corporate taxation and related-party transactions.
distractorHooks: Pricing of goods between unrelated competitors
Pricing set by the government for essential commodities
Pricing of shares during an Initial Public Offering

claimText: The Arm's Length Principle requires that prices in related-party transactions match the prices in comparable unrelated-party transactions.
evidenceExcerpt: The Arm's Length Principle states that prices in related-party transactions must match prices in comparable unrelated-party transactions.
examUse: Concept-based question on international taxation principles.
distractorHooks: Prices must be significantly lower than the market rate to avoid tax evasion
Prices are determined directly by the Central Board of Direct Taxes
Prices must be based solely on the cost of production without any profit margin
